mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an impending economic contraction, causing corporate earnings revisions downward and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between manufacturing output and consumer price inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks are resolving while demand remains sticky, compressing profit margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a rolling regression, or SPX forward returns are uncorrelated with the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL spread.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive federal funds rate hikes outpace inflation expectations, causing real interest rates to spike and equity risk premiums to expand, which suppresses S&P 500 valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Real rates rise but equity valuations hold steady due to earnings growth offsetting discount rate effects, or forward returns remain positive despite real rate spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures drives corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains positive but SPX forward returns are negative, or the signal fails to predict returns during high-volatility earnings seasons.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its long-term trend indicates robust capacity utilization and healthy corporate throughput, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as operational leverage boosts margins.
failure_condition: Industrial production remains elevated but SPX forward returns turn negative due to margin compression from input costs, or the threshold crossing yields no directional return bias.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects broad-based economic resilience, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as risk appetite expands.
failure_condition: Both labor and housing data improve but equity returns decline due to valuation multiple contraction, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp deceleration in real consumer spending combined with rising unemployment signals demand destruction, forcing downward earnings revisions and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: GDP growth slows but equity markets rally on liquidity injections, or the combined signal fails to outperform a baseline unemployment-only model in predicting downside returns.
